topics.title: GSC opportunity review: Last Z Redeem Codes — Active Codes, Gift Center Login, and UID
topics.source_reference: GSC weekly 2026-05-10: page opportunity and query-page signals
topics.notes: This is the clearest high-value signal. The page already exists as a cornerstone guide in the Economy cluster, and the GSC data shows meaningful impressions with weaker CTR on gift center related queries. The proposal also protects canonical claims and preserves cluster separation, which makes it suitable for a human review of an existing page rather than a new page. Prior run `2026-05-05-codes-gsc-opportunity-llm-approved-intake` is `closed`; keep this topic in monitoring unless new evidence materially changes the opportunity.
topics.player_value: Improves query-to-page match for players searching gift center and redeem code help, especially login and UID related intent.
topics.duplication_risk: Low if scoped to the current codes page and kept within the existing Economy cluster role.
claims_to_verify: Whether the low CTR queries reflect a true content gap or just ranking position effects.
Whether any copy changes would preserve gift-center-only-redeem-flow, gift-rewards-mailbox, and gift-center-cluster-role-separation.
Whether another canonical page already better satisfies the gift center login intent.
topics.next_step: Human owner review should confirm whether the page can be adjusted within current template and claim boundaries without expanding scope.
evidence: GSC page signal: codes.html had 33220 impressions, 586 clicks, 1.76% CTR, avg position 6.11.
Low CTR query: `last z gift center` had 3075 impressions, 113 clicks, 3.67% CTR, position 6.83.
Low CTR query: `last z gift center login` had 943 impressions, 43 clicks, 4.56% CTR, position 5.41.
Low CTR query: `lastz gift center` had 555 impressions, 23 clicks, 4.14% CTR, position 6.37.
Low CTR query: `last-z.com gift center` had 479 impressions, 22 clicks, 4.59% CTR, position 6.00.

site_fit.primary_user_job: Improve query-to-page match and first-screen usefulness for `last z gift center` searchers.
site_fit.archetype_reason: Existing page is `cornerstone-guide` in the `Economy` cluster; Scout should prefer updating the existing page before proposing new content.

constraints: Use existing page template and navigation patterns.
Treat GSC data as a signal, not proof that a rewrite is needed.
Protect canonical claim `gift-center-only-redeem-flow`.
Protect canonical claim `gift-rewards-mailbox`.
Protect canonical claim `gift-center-cluster-role-separation`.
Existing backlog history for this page: gift-center-ctr-pass:done.
reject_if: The query intent is already better served by another canonical page.
The proposed change would blur cluster role separation.
The improvement cannot be expressed without changing a cornerstone page beyond the approved scope.

closeout.note: Owner-reviewed no-op proposal. Current codes.html first-screen and metadata already match the intended codes hub role; no content change applied.

topics.title: External source opportunity: HQ and progression requirement cross-check
topics.notes: This is a useful cross-check opportunity for HQ and progression planning. It is tied to an existing page, has a clear player job, and is explicitly framed as verification rather than copy sourcing. The topic is useful if it helps validate dependency coverage and avoids role drift.
topics.player_value: Helps players confirm HQ requirements and progression dependencies before they invest resources.
topics.duplication_risk: Medium because it may overlap with existing progression guidance if the distinct job is not kept narrow.
claims_to_verify: HQ requirement details and construction dependency order.
Whether the proposed coverage adds a distinct job beyond existing progression guidance.
Whether the source can be corroborated without using competitor wording.
topics.next_step: Verify the external claims against canonical memory and one additional reliable source before any content proposal is drafted.
evidence: Owner-approved wiki/reference source can reveal HQ and progression planning gaps.
External source URL recorded for later manual verification.

topics.title: External source opportunity: research cost and branch coverage cross-check
topics.notes: This is a strong verification-oriented opportunity for the Research cluster. The proposal focuses on branch coverage, cost-name drift, and planning gaps, which are valuable maintenance issues for an existing atlas page rather than a new content initiative. Prior run `2026-05-16-external-research-costs-external-cross-check-llm-approved-intake` is `closed`; keep this topic in monitoring unless new evidence materially changes the opportunity.
topics.player_value: Reduces the chance that players rely on stale research cost tables or incomplete branch coverage.
topics.duplication_risk: Medium because research cost topics often overlap with other mechanics pages unless the scope is tightly defined.
claims_to_verify: Research branch names and cost table accuracy.
Whether the external reference covers unique gaps not already handled elsewhere.
Whether the proposal can be expressed without copying source wording.
topics.next_step: Check canonical site memory and owner notes for the current research page scope before any proposal-only workflow starts.
evidence: Owner-approved research reference source can reveal branch coverage gaps and cost/name drift.
External source URL recorded for later manual verification.
